name: Mushroom Polenta
recipeInstructions:
1. Boil the water.
2. Drizzle the polenta into the swirling water.
3. Mix in the hot chile pepper and simmer for around 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
4. Pour the mixture into a loaf pan (glass) and let cool off.
Recipe:
5. Bake the basic polenta in an oven at 425°F (220°C) for 20-30 minutes, turning once.
6. Chop the mushrooms.
7. Preheat a small frying pan using medium heat. Add balsamic vinegar as if it were oil (more generous than with oil). Add mushrooms and stir. Cover and reduce heat to low for 10-15 minutes. Shake the pan occasionally to mix.
8. Steam the sun-dried tomatoes for around 5 minutes to rehydrate them.
9. Chop the sun-dried tomatoes and add them to the mushrooms. Add water or balsamic vinegar if necessary (the mushrooms release a lot of liquid during cooking).
10. Pour the "sauce" over the polenta and serve.
The tomato/mushroom mixture is not really a sauce - by the time it's done, there is little or no liquid left. It may be better as is, though.
The basic polenta should keep for up to a week in the refrigerator with no problem.
